nativeRange.habitat: Shallow, slow-moving to still freshwaters — rivers, streams, lakes, and ponds, often on sandy or silty substrates with sufficient light penetration.

decorPreferences: Fine sand or fine gravel substrate
Open background space for runner spread
Root tabs near the crown for heavy root feeders

summary: Vallisneria is a hardy, fast-growing background plant with long ribbon-like leaves, widely regarded as one of the most beginner-friendly aquarium plants available. It spreads readily by sending out runners across the substrate.
careNotes: Vallisneria spiralis is generally considered one of the most forgiving aquarium plants, tending to thrive in a wide range of community-tank conditions. It is commonly advised to plant the crown JUST ABOVE the substrate — burying the crown is a frequent cause of rot and failure, as the growing point needs access to light and water circulation. Once established, it spreads rapidly by sending out runners (stolons) across the substrate; each runner produces a daughter plant that roots independently, and in smaller tanks this growth tends to require periodic thinning to prevent the plant from overtaking the layout. Although CO2 is not required, growth rate and leaf coloration tend to improve noticeably with supplementation. Vallisneria is a heavy root feeder, so root tabs placed near the crown generally outperform water-column dosing. It has been reported to be sensitive to liquid carbon products (Excel-type glutaraldehyde additives) at higher doses, which can cause leaf melt, so these should be used cautiously if at all in a tank with Vallisneria. Note: this entry covers Vallisneria spiralis specifically; other commonly traded species — including V. americana, V. asiatica, V. gigantea, and V. nana — share a broadly similar care profile but differ in mature size and leaf width and should be researched individually.

primary.name: Tropica
primary.notes: Primary search URL did not surface a species page; substituted the Tropica article on V. spiralis 'Tiger' which covers the same species (just the colored cultivar).

additional.notes: Cross-checked height (~61 cm), temperature (21–28°C), lighting (medium), placement (background), and propagation via runners.

additional.name: Aquarium Breeder
additional.notes: Cross-checked water parameters (pH 6.5–8.0, gH 4–18, temp 20–28°C), family (Hydrocharitaceae), and runner-based propagation. Aligned with Tropica on easy care level.
